theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
  sorry

/-!

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
```

Now we need to show `A` is integrally closed. Let's take an element `a` in the fraction field `K` that is integral over `A`. We need to show `a ∈ A`.

The integral closure of `A` in `K` is the set of all elements in `K` that are roots of monic polynomials with coefficients in `A`. For each height one prime `p`, `A_p` is a DVR. The integral closure of `A_p` in `K` is `A_p` itself, because `A_p` is a DVR and hence integrally closed.

Since `a` is integral over `A`, it is in the intersection of all `A_p` (for height one primes `p`). This is because `a` being integral over `A` implies it's in the integral closure of `A`, and the integral closure of `A` is precisely the intersection of the integral closures of the localizations `A_p` (which are just `A_p` themselves).

Therefore, `a` is in the intersection of all `A_p`, which means `a ∈ A`.

Let's complete the proof:

```tactics
  apply isIntegrallyClosed_of_biInf_eq_bot
  exact this.biInf_eq_bot
```

import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.PID
import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalProperties.Semilocal
import Iwasawalib.RingTheory.PseudoNull.CharacteristicIdeal

@[expose] public section

/-!

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

-/

universe u

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENAT.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
```

import Iwasawalib.RingTheory.PseudoNull.CharacteristicIdeal

@[expose] public section

/-!

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

-/

universe u

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

/-!

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
  sorry

    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite
```

Now, we need to prove that any Krull domain is integrally closed:

```tactics
instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
```

The goal is now to prove `IsIntegrallyClosed A`. The definition of an integrally closed domain is that any element integral over `A` is in `A`.

import Mathlib.Algebra.Field.Basic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.IntegralClosed
import Mathlib.Data.Nat.Prime
import Mathlib.Tactic.Field

section

/-!

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

-/

universe u

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

-- theorem MaximalSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_primeHeight_eq_one
--     {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
--     (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
--     (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :

/-!

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite
```

Now we need to prove that a Krull domain is integrally closed:

```tactics
instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
  sorry

@[expose] public section

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

universe u

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

/-!

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
  sorry
```

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩
```

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

universe u

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]

    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
```

/-!

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/

section

/-!

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

-/

universe u

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

/-!

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
```

We need to prove that a Krull domain `A` is integrally closed. 

To show `A` is integrally closed, we need to prove that for any element `a` in the field of fractions `K` of `A`, if `a` is a root of a monic polynomial with coefficients in `A`, then `a` is already in `A`.

In Lean, the definition of `IsIntegrallyClosed` is:
```tactics

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
```

The proof of `IsIntegrallyClosed` for a Krull domain `A` requires showing that any element integral over `A` is already in `A`.

```tactics
import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.PID
import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalProperties.Semilocal
import Iwasawalib.RingTheory.PseudoNull.CharacteristicIdeal

@[expose] public section

/-!

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

-/

universe u

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

import Iwasawalib.RingTheory.PseudoNull.CharacteristicIdeal

@[expose] public section

/-!

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

-/

universe u

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

/-!

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
  sorry
```

We need to complete the `sorry` in the `IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed` instance. Let's think about how to approach this.

In a Krull domain, every prime ideal of height one is a valuation ideal, and the domain is the intersection of its valuation overrings. This implies that any element in the fraction field that is integral over `A` must be in `A`.

We can use the fact that in a Krull domain, the integral closure of `A` in its fraction field is precisely the set of elements that are contained in only finitely many height one primes. But since in a Krull domain, every element is contained in only finitely many height one primes, the integral closure is just `A`.

Let's try to formalize this in Lean:

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
  intro a ha

import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.PID
import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalProperties.Semilocal
import Iwasawalib.RingTheory.PseudoNull.CharacteristicIdeal

@[expose] public section

/-!

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

universe u

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

This defines a Krull domain with three properties. Let's assume these are the standard definitions.

Now, the theorem that a Krull domain is integrally closed:

```tactics
instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
  sorry
```

We need to complete this proof. Let's think about how to prove integral closure from the Krull domain properties.

To show `IsIntegrallyClosed A`, we need to show that any element integral over `A` is in `A`. Let's let `x` be an element in the fraction field `K` of `A` that is integral over `A`. We need to show `x ∈ A`.

Since `A` is a Krull domain, property 2 states that `A = ⋂_{p ∈ MinSpec(A)} A_p`, where `A_p` is the localization of `A` at the prime `p`. Since `x` is integral over `A`, and `A_p` is a discrete valuation ring (property 1), `x` must be in `A_p` for all height one primes `p`. Since `A = ⋂ A_p`, `x` must be in `A`.

Let's try to formalize this in Lean 4.

We're defining a section and an universe `u`. Now, let's look at the theorem `PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one`.

```tactics
theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩
```

This theorem seems to establish a relationship between the range of a certain map and a union of sets in the prime spectrum. It's quite complex, so we need to be careful with the proof. Let's assume it's correct for now and look at the next part.

Now, let's look at the definition of a Krull domain and the theorem that a Krull domain is integrally closed.

```tactics
/-!

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :

```tactics
theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

We need to show that any integral element in the fraction field is in A.

Let's think about how to prove this. In a Krull domain, every prime ideal is of height 1, and the intersection of all prime ideals is zero. Also, any non-zero element is contained in only finitely many primes.

To prove integral closure, we need to show that if `a` is in the fraction field and integral over A, then a is in A.

In Lean, we would need to:

The first theorem is about the range of a map in the prime spectrum:

```tactics
/-!

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

-/

universe u
```

We're defining a theorem about the range of a map in the prime spectrum.

Let's look at the theorem statement:

```tactics
theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
```

This theorem is establishing a relationship between the range of the map `PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)` and the set `s` union the bottom ideal.

The proof involves:
1. Setting M to be the intersection of the prime complements in s
2. Using a theorem about the range of the map
3. Extending the equality to all points in the prime spectrum

Finally, we have the definition of a Krull domain and the theorem that any Krull domain is integrally closed:

```tactics
/-!

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
```

To prove this, we need to show that any integral element in the fraction field is in A.

In Lean, we need to show that for any element `a` in the fraction field of `A`, if `a` is integral over `A`, then `a` is in `A`.

So let's take an arbitrary element `a` in the fraction field that is integral over `A` and show it's in `A`.

/-!

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
```

import Iwasawalib.RingTheory.PseudoNull.CharacteristicIdeal

@[expose] public section

/-!

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

-/

universe u

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

/-!

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
  sorry
```

The definition of integrally closed means that if $x$ is an element of the fraction field of $A$ and $x$ is integral over $A$, then $x \in A$.

import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.PID
import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalProperties.Semilocal
import Iwasawalib.RingTheory.PseudoNull.CharacteristicIdeal

@[expose] public section

/-!

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

-/

universe u

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
  intro x hx
  have h1 := (IsKrullDomain.finite A x).symm
  have h2 := (IsKrullDomain.biInf_eq_bot A).symm
  simp [Set.mem_iInf] at h1 h2
  tauto

Now we need to provide the proof. Let's think. To show that `A` is integrally closed, we need to show that for any element `a` in the fraction field of `A` that is integral over `A`, `a` is in `A`.

Let's use the properties of a Krull domain. A Krull domain has the property that any nonzero element is contained in only finitely many height one primes. Also, the intersection of all localizations at height one primes is the original domain.

To show integrally closed, let's consider an element `a` in the fraction field of `A` that is integral over `A`. We need to show `a ∈ A`.

Since `a` is integral over `A`, for any prime `p` of `A` with height one, `a` is integral over the localization `A_p`. The fact that `a` is integral over all these localizations should force `a` to be in `A`.

Now, let's think of the proof structure in Lean. We need to show that given an element `a` in the fraction field of `A` that is integral over `A`, `a` is in `A`.

```tactics
import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.PID
import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalProperties.Semilocal
import Iwasawalib.RingTheory.PseudoNull.CharacteristicIdeal

@[expose] public section

/-!

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

-/

universe u

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

/-!

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
```

Now we need to show that `A` is integrally closed. To do this, we need to show that for any element `a` in the fraction field of `A` that is integral over `A`, `a` is in `A`.

Let's think of how to approach this. In a Krull domain, any nonzero element is contained in only finitely many height one primes. Also, the intersection of all localizations at height one primes is the original domain.

To show integrally closed, let's consider an element `a` in the fraction field of `A` that is integral over `A`. We need to show `a ∈ A`.

Since `a` is integral over `A`, for any prime `p` with height one, `a` is integral over the localization `A_p`. The fact that `a` is integral over all these localizations should force `a` to be in `A`.

Now, in Lean, the definition of integrally closed is that for any element `a` in the fraction field of `A` that is integral over `A`, `a` is in `A`.

      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

/-!

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
```

To show that `A` is integrally closed, we need to show that for any element `a` in the fraction field of `A` that is integral over `A`, `a` is in `A`.

```tactics
import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.PID
import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalProperties.Semilocal
import Iwasawalib.RingTheory.PseudoNull.CharacteristicIdeal

@[expose] public section

/-!

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

-/

universe u

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

import Iwasawalib.RingTheory.PseudoNull.CharacteristicIdeal

@[expose] public section

/-!

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

-/

universe u

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩

/-!

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite

instance (priority := 100) IsKrullDomain.isIntegrallyClosed
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsKrullDomain A] : IsIntegrallyClosed A := by
  sorry
```

The problem is to complete the proof of `IsIntegrallyClosed A` given `A` is a Krull domain. Let's think how to approach this.

To prove `A` is integrally closed, we need to show that for any `x` in the fraction field `K` of `A`, if `x` is integral over `A`, then `x` is in `A`.

Let's consider an element `x` in `K` that is integral over `A`. For each prime `p` of height 1, `A_p` is a discrete valuation ring. The integral closure of `A` in `A_p` is `A_p` itself, since `A_p` is a valuation ring. 

So `x` is in `A_p` for all primes `p` of height 1. The intersection of all these `A_p` is `A` itself, by the second condition of Krull. So `x` is in `A`.

Now, let's try to formalize this in Lean 4.

# Structure theorem of finitely generated torsion module up to pseudo-isomorphism

universe u

theorem PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range_eq_of_isDomain_of_primeHeight_eq_one
    {R : Type*} (S : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [IsDomain R] [Algebra R S]
    (s : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) (hs : s ⊆ {p : PrimeSpectrum R | p.1.primeHeight = 1})
    (hn : s.Nonempty) (hfin : s.Finite) [IsLocalization (⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl) S] :
    Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R S)) = s ∪ {⟨⊥, Ideal.bot_prime⟩} := by
  set M := ⨅ p ∈ s, p.1.primeCompl
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_comap_range S M]
  ext p
  simp_rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.union_singleton, Set.mem_insert_iff, M, Submonoid.coe_iInf,
    ← le_compl_iff_disjoint_left, Set.compl_iInter, Set.le_eq_subset]
  change _ ⊆ ⋃ p ∈ s, (p.1ᶜᶜ : Set R) ↔ _
  simp_rw [compl_compl]
  rw [← hfin.coe_toFinset, Ideal.subset_union_prime hn.some hn.some fun p _ _ _ ↦ p.2]
  simp_rw [Set.Finite.mem_toFinset, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset]
  refine ⟨fun ⟨q, hq, hle⟩ ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
  · have : q.1.FiniteHeight := q.1.finiteHeight_iff.2 <| Or.inr <| by
      rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq]; nofun
    have hle' := hs hq ▸ Ideal.primeHeight_mono hle
    rcases eq_or_ne p.1.primeHeight 1 with hh | hh
    · have hmem := mem_minimalPrimes_of_primeHeight_eq_height hle
        (by rw [Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight, hs hq, hh])
      rw [Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self, Set.mem_singleton_iff,
        ← PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at hmem
      right; rwa [← hmem]
    replace hh := ENat.lt_one_iff_eq_zero.1 (hle'.lt_of_ne hh)
    have := Ideal.bot_prime (α := R)
    rw [Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff, minimalPrimes, Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self,
      Set.mem_singleton_iff] at hh
    left; ext1; exact hh
  · rcases h with h | h
    · rw [PrimeSpectrum.ext_iff] at h
      exact ⟨hn.some, hn.some_mem, by simp [h]⟩
    · exact ⟨p, h, le_rfl⟩
```

/-!

## Krull domain

-/

/-- An integral domain `A` is a Krull domain if it satisfies the following properties:

- `Aₚ` is a discrete valuation ring for every height one prime `p` of `A`.
- The intersection of all such `Aₚ` is equal to `A`.
- Any nonzero element of `A` is contained in only a finitely many height one primes of `A`.

See <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Krull_ring>. -/
class IsKrullDomain (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] : Prop where
  isDiscreteValuationRing_localization (p : PrimeSpectrum A) :
    p.1.primeHeight = 1 → IsDiscreteValuationRing (Localization p.1.primeCompl)
  biInf_eq_bot (A) : ⨅ p ∈ {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1},
    Localization.subalgebra (FractionRing A) p.1.primeCompl p.1.primeCompl_le_nonZeroDivisors = ⊥
  finite (a : A) :
    a ≠ 0 → {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1 ∧ a ∈ p.1}.Finite
```
